title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Purple Coneflower tea, including quercetin and kaempferol, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and discomfort, soothing itchy eyes.

Antihistamine activity: The tea's antihistamine properties, primarily attributed to the presence of apigenin, help to alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergies or histamine reactions, providing relief from itching, burning, and redness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, help reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals and promoting healing in the eyes. As a natural remedy, it can provide quick relief from itchy, watery, and red eyes, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids (e.g., Cichoric acid, Isoorientin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with itchy eyes.
Phenolic acids (e.g., Chlorogenic acid, Caffeic acid): These antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to inflammation and irritation in the eyes.
Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergic reactions, including itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea contain anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help relieve itchy eyes and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh flowers of echinacea purpurea early in the morning to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Clean the harvested flowers thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop 2-3 tablespoons of the cleaned flowers into small pieces using sharp scissors or a herb chopper.
Steep the chopped flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and let it cool to room temperature before serving.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, including vitamin A and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate itchiness and discomfort.

Calming and soothing properties: Pot marigold tea's calming compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and terpenoids, have a natural sedative effect on the eyes, helping to reduce itchiness, irritation, and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The antioxidants present in Calendula officinalis reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or minor irritations. The tea's ability to calm and protect the eyes makes it a popular natural remedy for promoting eye health and comfort. Regular consumption may also help to prevent future itchy eye episodes.
remedy_constituents: Neryl acetate: This terpene helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy eyes by modulating the immune response and promoting relaxation.
Saponins: These glycosides exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing swelling and suppressing allergic reactions.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, Quercetin helps soothe itchy eyes by reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: They are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to calm itchy eyes.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula flowers when they are in full bloom and the petals are fully opened.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours or by air drying them for 7 to 10 days in a warm dry place.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water needed for the tea.
Steep the dried calendula flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes or longer to your desired strength.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then let the tea cool before serving.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties in aloe tea help combat infections that can cause itchiness and inflammation in the eyes, promoting a healthy environment for vision.
Soothing and moisturizing: Aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects calm and hydrate the eyes, reducing itchiness and dryness caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or allergies.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated eyes. The gel extracted from the aloe plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which work together to reduce swelling and itching. As a natural remedy, aloe vera tea can provide quick relief from itchy eyes, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural and gentle solution. Its cooling effects can provide instant comfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosidic compounds are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and reduce irritation in itchy eyes.

Flavonoids (Chalcones and Flavonols): As powerful antioxidants, flavonoids help to neutralize free radicals that may contribute to inflammation and irritation in itchy eyes, promoting comfort and relief.

Saponins (Triterpenes and Sterols): Saponins possess anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help to reduce swelling, soothe itchy eyes, and protect against infections that may exacerbate the condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy eyes and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers contain aloin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate itchy eyes and promote healing.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside Aloe vera leaves contains hyaluronic acid, which can help soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-12 fresh aloe vera leaves and rinse them under cold water for 5 minutes.
Remove the outer skin of the aloe vera leaves and chop them into small pieces weighing about 20 grams.
Steep the aloe vera pieces in 250ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release their properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Store the herbal aloe vera tea in a glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the eyes, alleviating itchiness and redness.

Berberastain: Possesses antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate bacterial overgrowth in the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with conjunctivitis and other eye infections.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: Exert a relaxing effect on the eyes, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness caused by allergies or irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The berberine content in goldenseal has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause eye irritation and itchiness. By soothing and protecting the eyes, goldenseal tea can provide relief from itchy eyes, promoting a healthy and comfortable eye environment.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Hydrastine and berberine, two alkaloids found in Hydrastis canadensis, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce itchiness and inflammation in the eyes.
Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, in Hydrastis canadensis may help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and reducing histamine release.
Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside, in Hydrastis canadensis may help soothe and calm itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of mucous membranes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root is the primary part used for its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties to soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make teas for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching in the eyes.
Barks: The bark is sometimes used in teas to provide additional anti-inflammatory effects and to help alleviate itchy eyes due to its soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis root in the spring or fall when it's fresh.
Steep the root in 250ml of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Consume the tea 2-3 times a day as needed to reap its potential health benefits slowly.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in comfrey tea protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy eye symptoms and promote healing.

Soothing and anti-irritant: Comfrey's mucilages (gum-like compounds) create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, soothing irritated tissues, reducing itchiness, and calming dryness or irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea's active compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work to calm and reduce inflammation in the eyes. This leads to a decrease in itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy and balanced eye environment, and providing relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Allantoin has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and reduce the irritation associated with itchy eyes.

Furanocoumarins (specifically, scopoletin and isoscopoletin): These furanocoumarins have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to relax and soothe the muscles around the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Saponins: Saponins in Comfrey tea have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate the symptoms associated with itchy eyes, such as redness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their high concentration of allantoin, a compound that soothes and calms irritated eyes.
Flowers: They are used in teas as they are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate itchy eyes and other eye irritations.
Roots: They are used in teas due to their rich content of allantoin and other compounds that help to reduce swelling and soothe itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the symphytum officinale leaves in early morning after the dew has evaporated for the best flavor.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried symphytum officinale leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then serve the tea hot or iced immediately.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine activity: Eyebright tea's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by allergies, allergic reactions, or other irritants.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in eyebright tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Eyebright tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate itchy eye symptoms and slow down the healing process.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, teas helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The leaves of the Euphrasia officinalis plant contain flavonoids and tannins that work to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching sensations in the eyes. The antihistamine properties also help to combat allergic reactions, such as those caused by pollen or dust, that can lead to itchy eyes. remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (Specifically Euphrasin): These compounds help reduce inflammation and relieve itchy eyes by inhibiting histamine release and reducing the permeability of blood vessels.

Iridoid Glycosides (Specifically Euphrasioside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and inhibiting the release of histamine.

Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help soothe and calm itchy eyes by reducing the release of inflammatory mediators and promoting the repair of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Euphrasia officinalis leaves are most commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.

Flowers: The flowers of Euphrasia officinalis are also widely used, as they contain a range of bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy eyes by soothing the mucous membranes.

Roots: The roots of Euphrasia officinalis are used in some herbal remedies, as they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold water to remove dirt and impurities then gently pat dry with paper towels.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove the plant material.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea's antioxidants can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which may contribute to itchiness and irritation.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe itchy eyes.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried althaea officinalis roots weighing approximately 1-2 teaspoons per 8 ounce cup of water.

Steep 1-2 teaspoons of crushed althaea officinalis powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.

Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for itchy eyes due to their ability to act as an anti-allergic agent, soothing irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted supplier for best results.
Rinse the roots with 2 cups of water to remove impurities and debris.
Boil the water in a teapot or kettle for 5-7 minutes to achieve optimal temperature.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers according to package instructions or grow your own chamomile plants.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with itchy eyes because its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The tea's natural antihistamine agents also help to reduce histamine release, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, rosemary's decongestant properties help to alleviate redness and swelling, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dry air, or other irritants. By drinking this herbal tea, you can find quick and gentle relief from itchy eyes without harsh chemicals or artificial remedies.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: Rosemary tea's antihistamine property helps to reduce the itching and inflammation associated with allergic reactions, such as conjunctivitis.
Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory property soothes and calms irritated eyes, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by itchy eyes.
Antioxidant property: Rosemary tea's antioxidant property helps to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry, itchy, and tired eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have a calming effect on the skin and mucous membranes, making them an effective remedy for reducing itchiness and redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosemary leaves of high quality from a reputable source measuring one teaspoon per cup.
Boil fresh filtered water in a pot on the stovetop or electric kettle for three to five minutes.
Add the dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water and let steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before consuming it in moderation up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with itchy eyes because its natural anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's calming effect can help reduce redness and itchiness, providing relief from conditions like dry eye syndrome or allergic reactions. Lemon balm tea also contains antioxidants that may help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, drinking lemon balm tea regularly can promote overall eye health and provide natural relief for itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm tea's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antihistamine activity: The antihistamine compounds present in lemon balm tea can counteract allergic reactions that cause itching, redness, and watery eyes, offering fast and effective relief.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidants help protect the eyes from free radical damage and oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy eye function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing itchiness and discomfort.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which help soothe and calm irritated eye tissue, providing quick relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves or 3-4 grams of fresh leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Bring 8 ounces of distilled water to a boil and then reduce heat to a simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the melissa officinalis leaves to the hot water and let steep for 10-15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids within 30 seconds of serving time.
Drink the tea immediately, ideally 2-3 times a day, for up to 14 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's natural compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes. Additionally, fennel tea's antibacterial qualities help to eliminate any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to itchiness. As a result, drinking fennel tea regularly can provide relief from itchy eyes, leaving them feeling calm, clear, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Fennel tea's antihistamine properties help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing the release of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that causes itching and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can cause itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes and other eye problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant are anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Buds: Fennel buds contain flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and itching associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves and flowers in the morning or evening for optimal potency.
Rinse the fennel leaves and flowers thoroughly with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried fennel leaves and flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it in sips, as needed, throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway teas helps with itchy eyes because its soothing properties calm irritated eye tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, work to alleviate redness and itchiness associated with dry eyes, allergic reactions, or conjunctivitis. Additionally, the herbal tea's gentle astringent properties help to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, providing relief from itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Caraway tea's antihistamine properties help to reduce itchiness and inflammation caused by allergic reactions, making it an effective remedy for relieving itchy eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Caraway tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy, red, or irritated eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Caraway tea's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye dryness, itchiness, and irritation, promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Seeds: Contain oil-rich fatty acids that lubricate and moisturize the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi seeds and wash them gently under cold running water.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried carum carvi seeds or two teaspoons of fresh seeds for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured seeds with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the seeds and discard them.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it slowly and steadily while keeping your eyes closed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry teas helps with itchy eyes because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and vitamins. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itching caused by allergies or dry eye syndrome. The antioxidants in highbush cranberry also help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate itchiness, providing long-lasting relief from uncomfortable and distracting symptoms. With regular consumption, highbush cranberry tea can provide a natural solution for itchy eyes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in highbush cranberry tea may help soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Antihistamine-like properties: Highbush cranberry tea contains flavonoids that can help stabilize mast cells, which are responsible for releasing histamine, a chemical that contributes to allergic reactions and itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Seeds: Highbush cranberry seeds contain omega-3 fatty acids, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves.
Boil one cup of water in a medium-sized saucepan for seven minutes.
Add the dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves to the boiling water and steep.
Steep for fifteen to twenty minutes, then strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve.
Pour the tea into a heat-resistant cup and allow it to cool to room temperature.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal teas are various skin irritations, such as eczema and acne. Tea made from chamomile, calendula, and peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin and reduce itching and redness. Additionally, herbal teas like burdock root and dandelion tea can help to calm and balance the digestive system, which may also contribute to itchy eyes.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy eyes are a natural remedy that helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes. They work by reducing inflammation and providing antioxidant properties to alleviate itchiness and discomfort. The best herbal teas for itchy eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Achillea millefolium, Aloe vera, Chamomilla recutita, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, itchy eyes can disrupt daily activities, causing fatigue, difficulty concentrating, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with itchy eyes is a blend of calendula, chamomile, and eyebright teas. Calendula tea soothes and calms irritated eyes, while chamomile tea reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation. Eyebright tea, rich in antioxidants, helps to reduce puffiness and ease itching. By combining these three teas, you can create a natural remedy to alleviate itchy eyes and promote overall eye health. Drink 1-2 cups a day, or use cooled tea bags as a compress for added relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis teas helps with itchy eyes because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, while also blocking the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching and irritation. By soothing and calming the eyes, Euphrasia officinalis teas can provide relief from itchy, irritated eyes, promoting clear vision and comfort. Its natural properties make it a popular remedy for eye health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes, thereby alleviating itchiness associated with eye irritations.
Flavonoids: The antioxidant properties of flavonoids help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
Phenylethanoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to calm the eyes and reduce itchiness caused by irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their rich content of alkaloids, which provide the medicinal properties for treating itchy eyes.
Flowers: Flowers are another key component used in teas to alleviate itchy eyes, as they contain a high concentration of euphrasine, which has anti-inflammatory properties.

Roots: Roots are sometimes used in combination with other parts to create a comprehensive tea for itchy eyes, due to their slightly lower concentration of active alkaloids compared to leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the harvested material under cold running water to remove dirt and other contaminants thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned material in a warm dry place for 2 to 3 days or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 3 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried euphrasia officinalis in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then let it cool to room temperature before drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant contains flavonoids and triterpenoids that have been shown to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes. Calendula's natural antiseptic properties also help to combat bacterial and viral infections that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing discomfort. This makes Calendula a popular natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: Help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Act as potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents to soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Naphthoquinones: Possess antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce itching and redness in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part to make teas due to their high anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make teas for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to calm itchy eyes.
Stems: The stems are another part of the plant used to make teas, although less commonly than flowers and leaves, to take advantage of their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers.
Steep the flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey or sugar to taste and stir well.
Drink the tea immediately and store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale teas helps with itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The tea is rich in compounds like allantoin, which helps to reduce swelling and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Symphytum officinale also help to alleviate itchiness and discomfort in the eyes. By drinking the tea, individuals can potentially experience relief from itchy eyes and promote overall eye health. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to address eye problems.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Saponins: These natural surfactants have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to calm and protect itchy eyes.
Caffeic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant properties, caffeic acid can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for itchy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Stems: Used to make teas for itchy eyes due to its ability to reduce swelling and inflammation.
Roots: Used to make teas for itchy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and stems of symphytum officinale in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried plant material for every 8 ounces of water used.
Steep the plant material in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain and discard the plant material after the steeping time to enjoy the tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The Echinacea plant contains compounds that soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, its immunomodulatory effects support the body's natural defense mechanisms, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with itchy eyes, such as redness and swelling. This natural remedy can be a welcome alternative to conventional treatments for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and soothe itchy eyes.
Cichoric acid: A type of phenolic compound, cichoric acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate itchy eyes and protect the surrounding tissues.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., echinacoside): These constituents have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response to alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for itchy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Used to make teas for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects that can help alleviate itchy and irritated eyes.
Roots: Used to make teas for itchy eyes because they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate itching associated with eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried echinacea purpurea root and 10-20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flower.
Combine the dried echinacea purpurea root and flower in a clean glass container.
Pour 250 milliliters of boiling water over the mixture and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Store the herbal tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and serve chilled.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids that work together to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing irritation and inflammation. remedy_constituents: Chlorogenic acid: It helps with itchy eyes by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and redness associated with eye irritation.

Apigenin: It helps with itchy eyes by acting as an antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to eye itchiness and inflammation.
Luteolin: It helps with itchy eyes by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes and alleviating symptoms of eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used to make teas for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make teas to treat itchy eyes due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.

remedy_recipe: Harvest achillea millefolium flowers and leaves by hand when they are in full bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves per 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the dried herbs in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain and serve the tea immediately while the herbs are still hot to preserve the medicinal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce redness and calm irritated eyes. The gel's active compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to have a cooling effect on the eyes, providing quick relief from itchiness and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: These help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing inflammation and itching caused by allergies or irritants.
Polysaccharides: These exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and alleviate itchy eyes by suppressing the immune system's response.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe the eyes, thereby alleviating itchy eyes.

Flowers: Aloe vera flowers are used to make teas that help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Aloe vera roots are used to make teas that are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 leaves of aloe vera and rinse them thoroughly with cool water for 5 minutes.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and soak them in 1 liter of cold water for 8 hours.
Strain the aloe vera liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a large pot for 10 minutes.
Boil the aloe vera liquid in a pot over medium heat for 10-15 minutes or until reduced slightly.
Allow the herbal aloe vera tea to cool and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita teas helps with itchy eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of itchy eyes, often caused by allergies or dryness. By relaxing the muscles around the eyes and reducing histamine release, Chamomilla recutita tea provides quick relief from itchy, watery, and irritated eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with itchy eyes by acting as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, reducing swelling and soothing the irritated tissues.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to calm and stabilize the histamine release in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and calm down the irritated tissues, reducing itchiness and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because they contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Leaves: They are used because they possess similar properties to the flowers, offering relief from itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Stems: They are used because they can be combined with the flowers and leaves to create a tea that offers a comprehensive approach to alleviating itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried flowers of chamomilla recutita at a ratio of 1 part herb to 8 parts boiling water.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solid particles.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired to enhance the flavor and aroma.
Drink the tea immediately to reap the potential benefits of the herbal infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The St. John's Wort herb contains flavonoids and terpenoids that reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes. Naphthodianthrones: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help soothe and calm itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for itchy eyes as they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe eye irritation.

Flowers: They are used to make teas for itchy eyes as they are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye inflammation and irritation.
Buds: They are used to make teas for itchy eyes as they contain flavonoids and other active compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of fresh flowers or 1/4 cup of dried flowers of the plant.
Chop the harvested flowers into small pieces using sharp scissors or a knife.
Steep the chopped flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Store the cooled tea in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and magnesium, which work together to calm and moisturize the skin and mucous membranes around the eyes. This helps to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, providing quick relief from itchy, watery, or irritated eyes. remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin around the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Anthraquinones: Relieves itchy eyes by reducing redness and itching due to its anti-irritant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Saponins: Soothes itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes due to its anti-inflammatory and emollient properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Flowers: Aloe barbadensis flowers are used in teas to provide their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate itchy eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 to 7 fleshy leaves from mature aloe barbadensis plants with sharp scissors or a knife.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in a large bowl of cold water for 30 minutes.
Blend the soaked aloe pieces with 1 cup of fresh water in a blender or food processor for 2 minutes.
Strain the aloe mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large pot for 10 minutes.
Bring the strained aloe mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce the heat to low for 15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The tea's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy and irritated eyes. Additionally, the tea's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to itchy eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa flowers in the early morning when they are still dewy for best flavor and aroma.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried melissa flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or iced depending on your preferred temperature and flavor.
